original_prompt:
can you complete this message: Hi Francesco,

I hope you're doing well! I've gone through all the points you shared and wanted to give you a full update.

✅ Resolved Issues
I've reviewed and fixed the issues you reported. Most of them are now resolved — please do another round of testing when you get a chance and let me know if anything still doesn't look right.

⚠️ Regarding the Permission Module (Points 7, 8 & 9)
I'd like to clarify something important here. The permission module is actually working correctly — I've tested it myself and the enable/disable functionality is behaving as expected.

The key thing to note is that at the top of the Access Management section, there is a company selector. Permissions are managed per company, so you need to first select the correct company before making any changes. It's possible that during your testing, a different company was selected, which would explain why the changes didn't seem to apply. Please give it another try by making sure the right company is selected first, and I'm confident it will work as expected.

📋 Leave Management Flow
Regarding the Day Off discussion — I wanted to also share the full leave approval flow so we're aligned on how the system works end to end:

Leave Approval Chain:
• Employee → Store Manager → Area Manager → HR
• Store Manager submitting → Skips Store Manager step → Area Manager → HR
• Area Manager submitting → Skips Store Manager & Area Manager steps → HR
• HR submitting → Goes directly to Admin / Super Admin
• Admin → Auto-approved

Additionally, the following gaps in the leave system are already on my radar and I'm working on addressing them:
1. Approver on leave – If the Store Manager is on leave, the request will automatically escalate to the next person in line.
2. No reminder/escalation – If a request is ignored for a set number of days, it will auto-escalate to the next approver with a notification.
3. Employee cancellation – Employees will be able to cancel their request as long as it hasn't been approved yet.
4. Too many employees off simultaneously – A warning will appear (or approval will be blocked) if too many staff are already off on the same dates.
5. Emergency HR/Admin approval – HR and Admin will have the ability to step in and approve at any stage if needed.
6. Leave for past dates – Requests submitted for already-passed dates will be routed directly to HR for quick handling.

🚧 Phase 3 – Work in Progress
Finally, I've started working on Phase 3, which includes: ATS (Applicant Tracking), Document Management, Notifications, Automation, and Onboarding. These modules are not yet complete, so please ignore them for now during testing. Once they're ready, I'll let you know and share them for review. In the meantime, if you have any early suggestions or ideas for these areas, I'd love to hear them!

Thanks as always for your detailed feedback — it's incredibly helpful. 🙏 here is the whole context:

Hi Usman, as I continue with the testing, I’ve collected a few points and I’m sharing them below in an organised way, so you can review them calmly when you have the time.
1) “Affluenza” button – data source The Affluenza button is present, but it’s not clear which data source it currently uses. At the moment, it doesn’t seem to be based on real data. To obtain accurate results, it will likely need to be connected to actual data sources (for example attendance, sales, or foot‑traffic databases). When you have time, please let me know how this part is intended to work.
2) Shifts – Terminal appearing in the list When assigning a shift to an employee, the Terminal also appears in the list. It would be helpful to clarify whether the terminal should be excluded from shift planning or if it has a specific role in this phase.
3) Area Manager – Transfers In the Area Manager role, within the Transfers section, the employee list does not appear even though the feature is enabled in the Admin permissions. It seems that, in this case, the permissions are not being applied correctly.
4) Store Manager – Shift templates The Store Manager should only be able to see and use shift templates associated with their own store, not templates from other stores.
5) Shifts – Day off In the shift planning, there is currently no way to manage a day off. It would be useful to explicitly mark a day when an employee is OFF.
6) Clock‑in without connection I tried to perform a clock‑in without network/data connection, but I was not able to do so. I’m not sure whether this is the intended behaviour or if it should be handled differently; if needed, I can share a screenshot.
7) Access Management – Attendance disabled but still visible From Admin → Access Management, I disabled the Attendance module for both Area Manager and Store Manager, but the module still appears. It seems the deactivation is not being applied correctly.
8) Anomalies – Enabled but not visible On the other hand, I enabled the Anomalies module for Area Manager, but it does not appear in the role’s view. Here too, there seems to be an inconsistency in how permissions are handled.
9) Access Management – Inconsistent behaviour More generally, in the Access Management section, I’ve noticed that some enable/disable toggles do not behave consistently. In some cases, modules remain visible even when disabled; in others, they do not appear even when enabled. It may be worth doing an overall review of the permission logic.

2 files

Hi Usman, I understand your point, and I’d like to share how I see this from an operational perspective.
For me, the day off fits better as part of shift planning, rather than being handled through leave management. It’s not an absence, but simply indicates that the employee is not scheduled to work on that specific day.
The idea would be to set a day directly in the shift calendar as OFF / Rest, without involving the leave or permission module and without generating anomalies or requests from the employee.
This would keep the workflow simple and clear for everyone involved.
Let me know what you think about this approach. Thanks as always for your support.

Usman Khalid
Apr 8, 2026 | 5:45 PM
for this: 5) Shifts – Day off In the shift planning, there is currently no way to manage a day off. It would be useful to explicitly mark a day when an employee is OFF.



I think it been handling from leave management.

Show more
Usman Khalid
6:03 PM
Thanks for sharing your perspective that makes sense from an operational point of view, and I agree that keeping it simple is important.



From the system side, this is actually already how it behaves: if no shift is assigned to an employee on a given day, it naturally means they’re not scheduled to work. So in practice, that day is already treated as an “off” day without needing to create anything in the leave module.



Because of that, introducing a separate “OFF/Rest” status in the shift calendar might duplicate what the system is already handling by default.



That said, if the goal is more about visibility (e.g., clearly marking planned rest days vs. just no shift assigned), we could explore a lightweight way to indicate that without adding complexity to the workflow.



Let me know your thoughts 👍

FP
